title: A Note on Boosting Uncloneable Encryption in Microcrypt
abstract: In this note, we consider the setting of uncloneable encryption satisfying uncloneable indistinguishability, a form of symmetric key encryption that prevents the cloning of ciphertexts in a very strong sense. Our goal is to minimize the assumptions under which (many-time secure) uncloneable encryption is known to exist, assuming the existence of an information-theoretic "uncloneable bit", i.e. a one-time secure uncloneable encryption scheme for one-bit messages. We observe that if a t -> t' uncloneable bit exists, then the following implications hold.   1. If many-time secure symmetric key encryption exists, then many-time secure t -> t' uncloneable encryption for arbitrary-length messages exists. Since many-time secure uncloneable encryption implies many-time secure symmetric key encryption, this result is tight.   2. If pseudorandom unitaries exist, then many-time secure t -> t' uncloneable encryption for arbitrary-length messages with identical copy security exists.   These results together show that many-time secure uncloneable encryption may follow from concrete assumptions in "microcrypt", the world of unstructured quantum cryptography that plausibly exists even if P = NP.
summary: This paper demonstrates how to transform simple one-time uncloneable bits into more powerful, reusable uncloneable encryption schemes for long messages. It establishes that many-time uncloneable encryption is essentially as easy to achieve as many-time symmetric key encryption, provided an uncloneable bit exists.
main_problem: The paper aims to minimize the cryptographic assumptions required to achieve many-time (reusable) uncloneable encryption (UE) in the 'microcrypt' setting.
main_result: The authors prove that if a $t 	o t'$ uncloneable bit and reusable symmetric key encryption exist, then many-time $t 	o t'$ uncloneable encryption for arbitrary-length messages also exists.
method: The authors use two main compilers: a plaintext expansion method using Decomposable Quantum Randomized Encoding (DQRE) and a purification channel method utilizing pseudorandom unitaries.
model_or_system: The work operates in the 'microcrypt' framework, a model of unstructured quantum cryptography that remains secure even if P = NP, utilizing quantum channels, symmetric key encryption, and uncloneable bits.
important_parameters: Security parameters $\lambda$, number of copies $t$ and $t'$, and the distinction between one-time and many-time security regimes.
main_assumptions: The existence of an information-theoretic $t 	o t'$ uncloneable bit, the existence of many-time secure symmetric key encryption, and the existence of pseudorandom unitaries (for normal form and identical copy security).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of boosting uncloneable encryption, presents a construction for expanding bit-size via DQRE, provides a second construction for achieving normal form and identical copy security using pseudorandom unitaries, and concludes with implications for the microcrypt setting.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
